title: Marbella Port Speedboat Rental Hourly: Costs, Seasons & Tips 2026
html:
Renting a speedboat by the hour in Marbella Port, departing from Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, typically costs between 180 and 350 EUR per hour. BoatHire24 offers a straightforward way to find and book hourly charters from local owners.

How much does it cost to rent a boat by the hour in Marbella Port?

Hourly rates for a private speedboat in Marbella Port range from 180 to 500 EUR depending on the boat model and season. You can compare verified boat listings on BoatHire24 to see exact prices for different vessels. Lower rates are typically available for longer bookings, such as four-hour slots.

Where to set off in Marbella Port

Most hourly rentals depart from Puerto Deportivo de Marbella, the main marina in the centre. Other departure points include Puerto Banús, a short drive west, and Cabopino Marina to the east. From Marbella Port, popular cruising grounds include the coast towards the La Concha mountain backdrop and the small bay of El Duque. You can also head south to sight the Moroccan coast on a clear day.

Which boat to rent for an hourly charter

BoatHire24 lists several boat types that are ideal for hourly hire in Marbella Port. Choose based on your group size and preferred experience.

  1. RIBs and small speedboats. Best for flexibility and speed; typically seats 6, 8 people; from 180 EUR per hour.
  2. Bowrider boats. Ideal for sunbathing and swimming; seats 6, 8; from 200 EUR per hour.
  3. Day cruisers. More comfortable with a cabin; seats 6, 10; from 300 EUR per hour.
  4. Sports boats such as the Olympic 490 for hire. A nimble, open-bow boat perfect for two to four people; from 220 EUR per hour. See the Olympic 490 for hire on BoatHire24 for details.

What are the seasons and licence requirements?

Book your hourly rental in advance during July and August, when Marbella Port is busiest and the afternoon levante wind often picks up, making morning trips more comfortable.

Peak season runs from June to September, with July and August being the most expensive months. If you plan to visit in 2026, consider May or October for lower rates and calmer seas. Regarding licensing: in Spain, private boat rental without a skipper generally requires a PER (Patrón de Embarcaciones de Recreo) licence for boats over 6 metres, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. Most hourly rentals include a skipper, so no licence is needed.

At a glance

  • Price range: 180, 500 EUR per hour
  • Peak months: June to September (July, August highest)
  • Trip length: 1 to 8 hours typical
  • Capacity: up to 10 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

How does booking work on BoatHire24?

Use the boat search on BoatHire24 to enter Marbella Port, your dates, and desired hours. You can filter by price, boat type, and whether a skipper is included. Each listing shows availability and the total cost, and you can book directly through the platform.

Questions about boat rental in Marbella Port

Can I rent a boat for just one hour in Marbella Port?

Yes, many owners offer one-hour bookings, although two-hour minimums are more common for speedboats.

Do I need a skipper for hourly rental in Marbella Port?

Most hourly rentals include a skipper who handles navigation and safety, but you can also rent bareboat if you hold a valid Spanish recreational licence.

What is the best time of day for a speedboat trip?

Morning trips (09:00, 12:00) are usually calmer and cooler, especially during the levante wind in summer afternoons.

Can we anchor and swim on an hourly rental?

Yes, many itineraries include a stop at a nearby cove such as Cabopino beach or the waters off Puerto Banús for a short swim.

BoatHire24 makes it easy to rent a boat in Marbella Port or rent out your boat on your own schedule. Whether you are a first-time renter or an experienced boater, the platform connects you directly with local owners.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella Port and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
